title: How to read a test step
goal: Learn what an action is, what an expected result is, and the difference between them.
preconditions: The Practice App is open below this case.

steps.action: Read the next two lines carefully before doing anything.
steps.notes: Every test step has TWO parts. The action tells you what to do. The expected tells you what should happen. You always check the expected against what you actually see. If they don't match, that is a bug — even if it seems small.

expected_overall: You clicked Add 1 three times and the counter ended on 3.
pass_criteria: The counter shows 3 after three clicks.

title: How to record a passing result
goal: Practice marking a case as passed.
preconditions: The counter shows 3 from the previous case.

steps.action: Click "Reset" to set the counter back to 0.
When everything matches what was expected, mark this case as passed using the runner controls.
steps.expected: The case is recorded as passed.

expected_overall: The counter went 0, -1, and you marked the case passed in the runner.
pass_criteria: The counter behaved correctly AND the runner recorded a pass.

title: How to record a failing result and write a useful note
goal: Practice marking a case as failed and writing a note someone else can read later.

steps.action: Click the button labeled "Crash on purpose."
steps.expected: The counter shows a number (any number).

steps.action: Look at the counter. Compare what you actually see to the expected result above.
steps.expected: You notice the counter is showing something that is not a number.

steps.action: Mark this case as failed. When the runner asks for a note, write what you actually saw. Be specific.
steps.expected: The case is recorded as failed with your note attached.

expected_overall: You correctly identified the mismatch, marked the case failed, and wrote a useful note.
pass_criteria: Case is recorded as failed AND the note describes what was seen, not just "doesn't work".
